LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED WITH A PROPER PROOF OF SERVICE. THE PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY THE BANK SHOWS THAT THE DEMURRER PAPERS WERE SERVED ON MS. BARTON AT 1049 HOWARD STREET. HOWEVER, PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE DEMURRER MS. HOWARD FILED A NOTICE OF THE CHANGE OF HER ADDRESS AND ALSO PROVIDED AN EMAIL ADDRESS. THERE IS NO INDICATION THAT MS. BARTON WAS SERVED AT HER NEW ADDRESS OR BY EMAIL. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE AN OPPOSITION. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 19, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE AN OPPOSITION BY APRIL 5, 2017, AND DEFENDANT MUST FILE THE REPLY BY APRIL 17, 2017. IF THE OPPOSITION IS NOT FILED, THE COURT WILL SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dean Christopherson, Esq. (925-256-6677) of Dawe & Christopherson LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s demurrer to the entirety of the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Allison Barton is sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Barton fails to allege facts that Wells Fargo owed her a legal duty to prevent her alleged harm. Order and judgment are signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-26-2017 Continued To Aug-09-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2017 Continued To Jun-14-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ALLISON MICHELLE BARTON, TEL. NO. 1-415-323-8871, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. DEAN A. CHRISTOPHERSON OF DAWE & CHRISTOPHERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-925-256-6677 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with thirty days leave to amend. The first amended complaint is vague and largely unintelligible. Ms. Barton is given the opportunity, if she can do so in good faith, to file a second amended complaint, which preferably will be typed, to state in clear, simple and easy-to-read and easy-to-follow language what wrongful conduct she alleges that Wells Fargo committed against her and how she was harmed by that wrongful conduct. The Second Amended Complaint to be filed by May 3, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Continued To Apr-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allison Michelle Barton, in pro per, Tel. 415.323.8875, for plaintiffs;
Dean A. Christopherson, of Dawe & Christopherson, Tel. 925.256.6677, for defendant Wells Fargo.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. The Court vacates its tentative ruling to allow plaintiff an opportunity to file an opposition. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to April 19, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. Plaintiff must file an opposition by April 5, 2017, and defendant must file the Reply by April 17, 2017. If the opposition is not filed, the Court will sustain the Demurrer without leave to amend. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Continued To May-03-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Continued To Mar-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Continued To Jan-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Allison Barton in Propria Persona.
Dean Christopherson of Dawe & Christopherson LLP telephone number 925-256-6677 for Defendant Wells Fargo Bank & Security Co.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s demurrer to the complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. It cannot be determined from plaintiff Allison Barton's complaint the legal theory or theories she is relying on or the facts that support that theory or theories. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Barton is given leave to allege one or more legally and factually supportable cause of action against Wells Fargo. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled without prejudice to being refiled with a proper proof of service. The proof of service filed by the Bank shows that the demurrer papers were served on Ms. Barton at 1049 Howard Street. However, prior to the filing of the demurrer Ms. Howard filed a notice of the change of her address and also provided an email address. There is no indication that Ms. Barton was served at her new address or by email.
